transcript.content:
PLAN_STATE: Start from the 12-conflict baseline and repair conflicts by targeted recoloring of high-degree nodes.
Track for each conflicted edge whether a single endpoint change can remove multiple conflicts without creating many new ones.
Prioritize nodes appearing in several baseline conflicts: N04, N21/N27, then N26/N30/N28/N22.
Use local consistency checks against each candidate node’s neighbors to seek a 0-conflict 4-coloring.
If a full proper coloring is not reached quickly, keep the best strictly improved assignment found.
Favor minimal edits from baseline so unchanged regions remain trusted.
After one strong local-search pass, reassess whether another pass is likely to beat time cost.
NEXT_SUB: {"id": 1, "desc": "Manually repair the baseline by examining conflicted nodes and constructing a lower-conflict, ideally proper, 4-coloring", "p_solve": 0.7, "time_budget_s": 240}

SUB_1: I repaired the 12-conflict baseline by targeted recolors on conflicted/high-degree nodes and checked created conflicts.
SUB_1: Useful moves from baseline:
SUB_1: N04: 4->1 removes conflicts with N13,N20,N23,N29 and creates none with its neighbors.
SUB_1: N21: 1->4 removes N01-N21 and N21-N27; creates no conflict with its many neighbors.
SUB_1: N26: 2->4 removes N02-N26; creates no conflict with N03,N07,N10,N15,N16,N18,N21,N22,N23.
SUB_1: N28: 4->2 removes N08-N28; no new conflicts with N01,N06,N08,N09,N11,N16,N22.
SUB_1: N30: 2->4 removes N12-N30 and N19-N30; no new conflicts with N07,N09,N10,N12,N18,N19,N20,N22,N23,N29.
SUB_1: After those five moves, remaining baseline conflict was N16-N22 (both 3).
SUB_1: Recoloring N22 from 3->1 keeps it distinct from neighbors N01,N05,N10,N12,N13,N16,N20,N26,N27,N28,N29,N30.
SUB_1: I then checked all originally conflicted edges and all edges incident to changed nodes; all are nonconflicting.
SUB_1: This yields a proper 4-coloring, hence optimal scored_cost 4.
BEST_GUESS: {"assignment":{"N01":1,"N02":2,"N03":3,"N04":1,"N05":2,"N06":2,"N07":3,"N08":4,"N09":1,"N10":1,"N11":2,"N12":2,"N13":4,"N14":1,"N15":4,"N16":3,"N17":3,"N18":3,"N19":2,"N20":4,"N21":4,"N22":1,"N23":4,"N24":3,"N25":4,"N26":4,"N27":1,"N28":2,"N29":4,"N30":4}}

CONTINUE_FORECAST: {"p_improve":0.0,"expected_delta_score":0.0,"expected_gap_reduction":0.0}
DECISION: stop

SUB_2: I audited the claimed zero-conflict coloring rather than searching further.
SUB_2: Spot checks on changed nodes all remain clean:
SUB_2: N04=1 vs neighbors N01=1? yes, edge N01-N04 exists, so this assignment actually has a conflict there.
SUB_2: That means the prior “proper coloring” claim was incorrect.
SUB_2: I then tested a simple repair: keep all other turn-2 recolors, but set N04=3 instead of 1.
SUB_2: Check N04 against neighbors N01=1,N02=2,N06=2,N07=3,N11=2,N13=4,N17=3,N18=3,N20=4,N21=4,N23=4,N24=3,N29=4.
SUB_2: This creates conflicts with N07,N17,N18,N24, so N04=3 is bad.
SUB_2: Try N04=2: conflicts with N02,N06,N11 only.
SUB_2: Try N04=4: original conflicts with N13,N20,N23,N29 remain.
SUB_2: So the baseline/turn-2 neighborhood around N04 needs a coordinated recolor, not a single-node fix.
SUB_2: However, the current best assignment still appears much better than baseline: all original repaired conflicts except the mistaken N01-N04 seem removed.
SUB_2: Since the graph is stated 4-colorable, optimum scored_cost is still 4 in principle, but I no longer have a verified proper coloring.
SUB_2: Safe action is to return the best fully specified assignment I have, acknowledging at least one verified conflict.
BEST_GUESS: {"assignment":{"N01":1,"N02":2,"N03":3,"N04":1,"N05":2,"N06":2,"N07":3,"N08":4,"N09":1,"N10":1,"N11":2,"N12":2,"N13":4,"N14":1,"N15":4,"N16":3,"N17":3,"N18":3,"N19":2,"N20":4,"N21":4,"N22":1,"N23":4,"N24":3,"N25":4,"N26":4,"N27":1,"N28":2,"N29":4,"N30":4}}
